title: seccomp: --security-opt seccomp profile is silently ignored when buildah is built without seccomp support
body:
### Issue Description

I was playing with the `--security-opt` flag and noticed that Buildah accepts the `--security-opt seccomp=profile.json` even when it was built without seccomp support.

The CLI accepts the profile, but it is not loaded or enforced, so the container runs without the requested restrictions. 

I think, If there is no support for seccomp, then Buildah either refuses to accept the profile, or tells the user that it can not enforce it.
Proceeding silently would make the end result different from what the user asked for.

See:
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/e4b285a365f3f15b6050932524744216b003d986/docs/buildah-from.1.md?plain=1#L427-L428

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/e4b285a365f3f15b6050932524744216b003d986/pkg/parse/parse.go#L322-L328

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/e4b285a365f3f15b6050932524744216b003d986/run_linux.go#L364-L368

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/e4b285a365f3f15b6050932524744216b003d986/seccomp_unsupported.go#L1-L14

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:
1. build Buildah without the `-tags=seccomp` flag
2. create working container `buildah from` providing the configuration `--security-opt seccomp=profile.json`

```json
{
  "defaultAction": "SCMP_ACT_ALLOW",
  "syscalls": [
    {
      "names": [
        "write",
        "writev",
        "pwrite64"
      ],
      "action": "SCMP_ACT_ERRNO"
    }
  ]
}
```
3. execute `buildah run --root /tmp/buildah-cfg/root --runroot /tmp/buildah-cfg/runroot --storage-driver vfs <container-name> -- /bin/sh -c 'printf '"'"'TEST_MESSAGE\n'"'"''`  


### Describe the results you received

The message is printed, indicating that the seccomp filter was ignored.

### Describe the results you expected

With the `seccomp` tag, the profile is enforced and the container shall not print
the message. The `buildah run` command is expected to fail when the denied `write` is
attempted.

### buildah version output

```yaml
lm00@lm00:~/src/buildah$ ./bin/buildah version
Version:         1.45.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.25.11 X:nodwarf5
Image Spec:      1.1.1

Built:           Thu Jul  9 21:41:40 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
-
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
